your_book:
Tuesday was a rough session for Jaleel's book. The portfolio shed ground across the board, with the account sitting at $21,172 against an unrealized loss of $518 (–2.4%). The heaviest damage came from the two largest positions: ServiceNow (NOW) cratered –5.76% to $2,411, dragged by IBM-related software sentiment even though NOW's own fundamentals remain intact; and Adobe (ADBE) dropped another –4.26% to $3,458, extending its open loss to –10.0% as the AI-disruption narrative continues to override what are still solid numbers. Boston Scientific (BSX) fell –4.52% to $1,065 — compounding a –11.0% open loss on the back of its guidance slash — and Abbott (ABT) slid –3.42% to $2,579 despite being one of the few positions still in the green (+2.9%). The only meaningful green came from NVIDIA (+4.06%, $1,382, open gain now +15.4%) and Rocket Lab (+2.71%), though RKLB's thesis alignment remains the weakest in the book at a QARP of 40.

The drift problem is concentrated in three names. ADBE and BSX are both rated STRONG BUY yet carry open losses of –10% and –11% respectively, with price and thesis pulling in opposite directions. LULU (–2.48% today, –8.2% open) is sitting on a guidance-cut risk flag with the next read not until September 2 — a long time to hold a deteriorating consumer story. NOW's –5.76% session loss is the sharpest single-day hit in the book; at 11.4% weight and a high multiple, it remains the single biggest drawdown accelerant if software sentiment stays sour.

Three things to watch closely: ABT reports next on July 16 — two days out — with an EPS guide already trimmed and a mild risk flag live. BSX reports July 29 with mgmt guiding flat through Q3; that's a low bar but the stock keeps falling into it. And NOW reports July 22 — the outcome there will matter disproportionately given its weight. Cash is effectively zero ($75), so any repositioning requires a sale first.

attention.headline: –5.76% single-session drop; earnings July 22 incoming at top weight
attention.note: NOW is the book's largest position by weight (11.4%, $2,411) and took the hardest session hit today — with earnings just eight days out, Jaleel needs a clear pre-earnings stance given the high multiple and macro-beta risk flagged.

attention.headline: Largest holding down –10% open, narrative overriding strong fundamentals
attention.note: At 16.3% weight ($3,458) and a –10.0% open loss, ADBE is the single biggest drag on the book; the AI-moat fear and CFO exit show no sign of abating, and next earnings aren't until September 10 — a long wait with no near-term catalyst to close the gap.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, –11% open loss, reports July 29
attention.note: BSX is down –4.52% today and –11.0% from cost, with management already guiding flat through Q3; the July 29 print is a live risk event that could force a reassessment of the STRONG BUY verdict.

attention.headline: Reports Wednesday July 16 — EPS guide already trimmed
attention.note: Abbott reports in two days with a mild but live risk flag on EPS; Jaleel should decide now whether to hold through the print or trim, since there is no cash buffer to act after the fact.
